paper_definition_large_deviation_rate.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
paper_definition_large_deviation_rate.tex_statement: For a real sequence A indexed by sample size and a real number r, paper_definition_large_deviation_rate A r is exactly the paper large-deviation-rate predicate definition_large_deviation_rate A r.

source_corollary_lem_mallowsnorando.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_corollary_lem_mallowsnorando.tex_statement: For a finite Mallows model with q < 1 and a target W candidate, any finite randomized family of nontrivial K-approval rules is weakly dominated by some static proper-prefix cutoff, and that cutoff is optimal among static cutoffs for the Mallows top-W K-approval outcome rate.

source_proposition1_thm_consistency_tier.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_proposition1_thm_consistency_tier.tex_statement: For a finite ranking law and staged target prefix sets, assuming every stage has a cross-tier pair and no cross-tier top-prefix probabilities tie, strict top-prefix dominance of every target member over every nonmember at every cut is equivalent to every reasonable prefix-score rule learning the staged target sets, meaning the selection error probability tends to 0.

source_proposition2_thm_pairwiselearning.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_proposition2_thm_pairwiselearning.tex_statement: For a finite signal law and two score functions with nonnegative expected score gap, the pairwise scoring error probability either has exponential rate pairwiseScoringRate, or has exponential rate -log pZero for a positive zero-gap probability pZero, or is eventually zero.

source_proposition3_lem_pairwiselearning.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_proposition3_lem_pairwiselearning.tex_statement: For finite ternary approval score gaps taking values 1, 0, and -1 with probabilities pUp, pZero, and pDown and pDown <= pUp, the pairwise scoring error probability either has exponential rate approvalPairwiseRate pUp pDown or is eventually zero.

source_proposition4_thm_goal_learning_fi.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_proposition4_thm_goal_learning_fi.tex_statement: For finitely many relevant pairs under a finite signal law, if every pair has nonnegative expected score gap, then the unweighted sum of the pairwise finite-score-gap error probabilities has some extended exponential rate.

source_theorem1_lem_randomizebetterscori.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_theorem1_lem_randomizebetterscori.tex_statement: For any finite randomized family of reasonable prefix scoring rules whose weights are nonnegative and sum to 1, if the target winner set satisfies strict top-prefix dominance, then the weight-averaged static scoring rule is reasonable, learns the winner set with error probability tending to 0, and has a static finite outcome-learning rate at least as large as the randomized scoring rule rate.

source_theorem2_lem_randomizenotbetterap.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_theorem2_lem_randomizenotbetterap.tex_statement: For any finite randomized K-approval rule with nonnegative weights summing to 1 and any candidate pair, there exists a static component rule whose extended approval pairwise rate weakly dominates the randomized rule pairwise rate.

source_theorem_lem_mallowsnotWK_countere.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_theorem_lem_mallowsnotWK_countere.tex_statement: There exists a high-noise Mallows instance centered at the identity in the four-candidate setting such that the W-approval cutoff is not approval-rate optimal: some different cutoff has strictly larger finite outcome-learning rate.

source_theorem_lem_randomizebetterapprov.comment: Context-free Lean-to-TeX translation for the current PaperInterface row.
source_theorem_lem_randomizebetterapprov.tex_statement: In the constructed four-candidate W-selection ranking law, strict top-prefix dominance holds for the relevant cross-tier pairs, and the 50/50 randomized approval rule has finite outcome-learning rate strictly larger than the best static K-approval cutoff among the finite static family.

prompt_summary: Translate each Lean declaration from the Lean statement alone, with no paper context.
Preserve every visible variable, binder, hypothesis, domain condition, equivalence or implication direction, and conclusion.
Do not replace formulas with theorem labels, informal endpoints, or proof-route summaries.
